nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ram
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Raghu, Hari, Kewal, Mast, Mohan, Sadhu, Geetha, Paras, Satish, Vijay, Alka, Anil.  Jewish Yossi, Amnon, Asher, Avi, Erez, Haim, Itzhak, Moshe, Shira, Yaron, Yosef.

Indian: from a personal name based on Sanskrit rāma ‘pleasing, charming’, the name of one of the chief incarnations of the god Vishnu. In the northern states of India it probably evolved into a surname from use as the final element of a compound personal names such as Atmaram (with Sanskrit ātmā ‘soul’) or Sitaram . Among Tamil and Malayalam speakers who have migrated from their home states, it is a variant of Raman . In the epic poem Ramayana, Rama is the elder brother of Lakshmana (see Lakshmanan ). Compare Rama .

Dutch, German, and English (East Anglia): from Middle Dutch, Middle High German, Middle English ram ‘ram’, either in the sense ‘male sheep’ or denoting an implement such as a battering ram or pile driver. Compare Ramm .

variants.name: Rajaram
meanings: Indian: from a personal name based on Sanskrit rajarama ‘King Rama’. See Ram .

variants.name: Ramachandran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ravi, Anand, Bala, Subramanian, Narayanan, Ram, Sridhar, Vijay, Ajit, Anush, Dileep, Geetha.

Indian (Kerala, Tamil Nadu): from a personal name composed of Rama (name of one of the chief incarnations of the Hindu god Vishnu; see Ram ) + Sanskrit čandra ‘pleasant, shining; moon’ (see Chandra ) + the Tamil-Malayalam third-person masculine singular suffix -n. — Note: Since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, this name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Ramalingam
meanings: Indian (southern states): from a personal name based on a Sanskrit expression meaning ‘the divine light of Rama’ (see Ram ); lingam in Sanskrit denotes the phallic symbol, which also symbolizes life and light. — Note: Since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, this name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Ramanathan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ramesh, Mohan, Gopal, Ganesan, Janaki, Krishna, Kumar, Murali, Ram, Sriram, Swaminathan, Anuradha.

Indian (Kerala, Tamil Nadu): from a personal name derived from Sanskrit rāmanātha ‘lord Rama’, from rāma ‘pleasing, charming’ (see Ram ) + nātha ‘lord’, + the Tamil-Malayalam third-person masculine singular suffix -n. — Note: Since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, this name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Lakshmanan
meanings: Indian (Tamil Nadu): from a personal name derived from Sanskrit lakṣmaṇa ‘one who has auspicious marks’. In the epic poem Ramayana, Lakshmana is the heroic younger brother of Rama (see Ram ). — Note: Since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, this name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Sitaram
meanings: Indian: from a personal name composed of Sanskrit rāma ‘pleasing, charming’, the name of one of the chief incarnations of the god Vishnu (see Ram ), + Sita, the name of Rama's wife.

variants.name: Ramamurthy
meanings: Indian (southern states): from a personal name based on a Sanskrit expression meaning ‘manifestation of the god Rama’ (see   Ram  ). — Note: Since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, this name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Ramani
meanings: Indian (Gujarat): Sindhi name, from an ancestral personal name containing the element Ram + the suffix -ani ‘descendant of’ (see Ani ).
Albanian: from the Albanian Muslim personal name Raman (definite form Ramani), which is an altered form of Rrahman (see Rahman ).

variants.name: Raman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Shankar, Krishna, Anand, Ravi, Usha, Natarajan, Sankar, Sudha, Vijay, Anil, Balu, Ganapathy.

Indian (Kerala, Tamil Nadu): from a personal name derived from Sanskrit rāma ‘pleasing, charming’ (see Ram ) + the Tamil-Malayalam third-person masculine singular suffix -n. — Note: Since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, this name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Ramakrishna
meanings: Indian (northern states): from a personal name composed of Rama + Krishna, both being incarnations of the god Vishnu (see Ram and Krishna ). Compare Ramakrishnan .

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: Kathelijn Peter de Ram was born about 1435. She had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Emondt Henrick Emondtszone van Chaem. She died about 1507, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Kathelijn Peter de Ram est née environ 1435. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Emondt Henrick Emondtszone van Chaem. Elle est décédée environ 1507, à l’âge de 74 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Kathelijn Peter de Ram è nata il circa 1435. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Emondt Henrick Emondtszone van Chaem. Morì circa 1507, all'età di 74 anni.
